ain: noun. Alternative form of ayin (“Semitic letter”).
ait: noun. An island in a river, especially the River Thames in England.
ani: noun. Any bird of the genus Crotophaga in the cuckoo family.

anting: noun. The practice of some birds of rubbing live ants or occasionally other items into the feathers, possibly as a means of controlling
any: adverb. To even the slightest extent, at all.
anything: pronoun. Any object, act, state, event, or fact whatsoever; a thing of any kind; something or other.
ayin: noun. The sixteenth letter of many Semitic alphabets/abjads (Phoenician, Aramaic, Hebrew, Syriac, Arabic and others).
gait: noun. A manner of walking or stepping; a bearing or carriage while moving on legs.
gay: adjective. Homosexual: Only possessing sexual and/or romantic attraction towards people one perceives to be the same sex or gender as
ghat: noun. A descending path or stairway to a river; a ford or landing-place.
giant: noun. A mythical human or humanoid of very great size.
gin: noun. A colourless non - aged alcoholic liquor made by distilling fermented grains such as barley, corn, oats or rye with juniper berries;
ginny: adjective. Affected by gin; resembling or characteristic of gin.
git: noun. A silly, incompetent, stupid, or annoying person (usually a man).
gnat: noun. Any small insect of the order Diptera, specifically within the suborder Nematocera.
hag: noun. A witch, sorceress, or enchantress; a female wizard.
hang: verb. To be or remain suspended.
hant: noun. Alternative form of haunt, haint (“ghost”)
hanting: verb. present participle and gerund of hant
hat: noun. A covering for the head, often in the approximate form of a cone, dome or cylinder closed at its top end, and sometimes having a brim

hay: noun. Grass cut and dried for use as animal fodder.
haying: verb. present participle and gerund of hay
hin: noun. A former Hebrew liquid measure of volume (about 3.8 L).

hying: verb. present participle and gerund of hie

nag: noun. A small horse; a pony.
nan: noun. Synonym of maid: a servant girl.
night: noun. The time when the Sun is below the horizon when the sky is dark.

nit: noun. The egg of a louse.

tangy: adjective. Having a sharp, pungent flavor.
than: conjunction. Used in comparisons, to introduce the basis of comparison.
thin: adjective. Having little thickness or extent from one surface to its opposite.
thing: noun. That which is considered to exist as a separate entity, object, quality or concept.
thy: determiner. Possessive form of thou: that which belongs to thee; which belongs to you.
tin: noun. A malleable, ductile, metallic element, resistant to corrosion, with atomic number 50 and symbol Sn.
ting: interjection. Representing a high-pitched sharp sound like a small bell being struck.
tinny: adjective. Of or pertaining to or resembling tin.

tyning: verb. present participle and gerund of tyne
yang: noun. A principle in Chinese and related East Asian philosophies associated with bright, hot, masculine, etc. elements of the natural world.
yin: noun. A principle in Chinese and related East Asian philosophies associated with dark, cool, female, etc. elements of the natural world.
agy: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Southern Alta.
ahing: verb. present participle and gerund of ah
anight: adverb. In the night-time, at night.
hain: verb. To hedge or fence in; enclose; protect by hedging.
han: verb. plural simple present of have
hiya: interjection. An informal greeting, hi, hello.
ing: noun. A meadow, especially a low meadow near a river; water meadow.
inga: noun. Any of the genus Inga of small tropical trees and shrubs.
nina: noun. A hidden message revealed in the completed grid of a crossword.
tai: noun. A fish, the Japanese bream.

tangi: noun. A Maori dirge, or song for the dead.
tanh: symbol. The hyperbolic function hyperbolic tangent.

thig: verb. To solicit or receive, usually by begging; ask as alms.
tig: verb. To touch lightly or playfully; to trifle, dally.
tyg: noun. Alternative form of tig (drinking cup)
